references.title: Predicting students’ performance in e‑learning using learning process and behaviour data

extracted.Features: [Learner Reparation Behaviour, Knowledge Acquisiton Behaviour, Interactive Learning Behaviour, Learning Consolidation Behaviour]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
All dimensions of extracted features are Standardized.

For feature selection, authors use Variance Threshold. 

Features are “fused” by clustering - one feature represents the entire group.
extracted.Models: SVM-R, SVM-L, Naive Bayesm kNN-U, kNN-D, Softmax
extracted.Performance Metric: Best Model: {Accuracy: 0.97 - Naive Bayes-PBC, F1-Score: 0.98 - Naive Bayes-PBC, Cohen’s Kappa: 0.94 - Naive Bayes-PBC}
extracted.Comments:
Authors present a framework of different learning behaviours call PBMC.

The work takes 12 learning behaviorsfrom one course and uses them build feature aggregations. Authors compare this representation against representations created by previous authors in the literature.

Authors show that their representation is better when compared against that of other algorithms…
extracted.Limitations:
- Holdout Method

- Only tested in a single course of OULAD

- Only considers e-learning scenarios.

- Limited number of extracted behaviors.

- No early prediction

annotations.text: Assessing elearning quality is often contingent on using early warning systems to predict learning outcomes. To improve on current state fo the art, the authors propose 2 new methods: BCEP and BCP that perform feature extraction on behavioral attributes.
Results on OULAD show that there is some merit in the approach even though the authors are not explicit on whether they have an early warning system or not.
